name: Community Investment Corporation
description_short: Chicago-based CDFI providing affordable financing for multifamily housing acquisition, rehabilitation, and construction, plus property management training and grants for community development.
description_long:
Community Investment Corporation (CIC) is a community development financial institution (CDFI) based in Chicago that specializes in affordable housing finance and community revitalization. Founded to support housing preservation and development in underserved Chicago neighborhoods, CIC operates as both a lender and educational resource for property owners and developers seeking to build or preserve affordable rental housing.

CIC's primary offerings include low-cost financing programs for multifamily buildings (5+ units), construction loans, energy efficiency retrofits, mezzanine debt products, and targeted lending in specific neighborhoods like North Lawndale and Woodlawn. The organization also provides grants covering up to 50% of development costs in designated Tax Increment Financing (TIF) districts, SRO (Single-Room Occupancy) housing loans, and property management training courses. Their educational offerings include both online ($50) and in-person ($60) Property Management Training certificates that cover landlord-tenant law, fair housing, tenant selection, and property maintenance—programs that comply with City of Chicago, HUD, and lender requirements.

CIC distinguishes itself through mission-driven, flexible underwriting that accommodates borrowers traditional lenders reject, neighborhood-specific lending programs, and integrated educational support. They actively list properties for sale, maintain published success stories from borrowers, and release annual performance reports documenting their impact on affordable housing. Their willingness to structure creative financing solutions (as evidenced by borrower testimonials) and focus on Chicago's most vulnerable residents—including those seeking SRO units—reflects a commitment beyond standard commercial lending.

However, CIC's services are geographically limited to Chicago and certain neighborhoods within the city. Their loan products require substantial multifamily or development expertise; individual homebuyers seeking primary residence mortgages are not the target market. The property listings shown are limited in number, and loan terms, rates, and specific eligibility requirements are not published online, requiring direct contact with the organization.
pros: Low-cost financing specifically designed for affordable housing projects, with flexible underwriting accommodating borrowers traditional lenders reject
Grants covering up to 50% of development costs for eligible properties in designated TIF districts
Neighborhood-specific lending programs (North Lawndale, Woodlawn, SRO housing) targeting underserved communities
Integrated property management training with HUD and City of Chicago compliance, affordable at $50-$60
Mezzanine debt product allowing owners to stretch equity in high-cost markets
Energy efficiency retrofit financing to reduce operating costs for multifamily buildings
Published annual performance reports and borrower success stories demonstrating measurable community impact
cons: Services limited to Chicago area; not available for borrowers outside the city
Loan products focused on multifamily (5+ units) and development; individual homebuyer mortgages not offered
Loan terms, rates, and eligibility requirements not published online; requires direct contact for specifics
Limited property inventory shown; availability and pricing for listed properties varies
Requires significant real estate development expertise; not suitable for first-time individual homebuyers
best_for: Multifamily property owners and developers seeking affordable financing in Chicago neighborhoods
Real estate investors focused on affordable housing preservation and community development
Landlords and property managers seeking professional training and certification compliant with City of Chicago requirements
SRO and supportive housing developers serving Chicago's vulnerable and homeless populations
diagnosis: CIC is best for multifamily property owners, real estate developers, and landlords in Chicago seeking affordable financing and professional property management training with a mission-driven lender committed to community development. The main caveat is that CIC does not serve individual homebuyers seeking primary residence mortgages, operates only in Chicago, and requires substantial real estate expertise and development-scale projects (multifamily or construction).
services: Multifamily acquisition and rehabilitation financing (5+ unit buildings)
Energy and water efficiency retrofit loans for multifamily buildings
North Lawndale housing preservation and improvement loans
Mezzanine debt financing for high-cost market equity stretching
Woodlawn construction loans for owner-developer rehabilitation and new construction
SRO (Single-Room Occupancy) construction loans and grant assistance
TIF district development grants covering up to 50% of project costs
Online Property Management Training certificate course (self-paced, $50)
In-person Property Management Training certificate classes ($60, full-day workshops)
Property sales listings in Chicago neighborhoods
Annual performance and credit review reporting
Borrower success support and case management
